# Image

The content image component displays responsive images with optional captions in various layout contexts. It extends the base image atom with content-specific features like captions and width controls, making it suitable for use within articles, pages, and other content types.

## Interactive Preview

Use the controls panel below to experiment with the image component's properties.

<Canvas of={ImageStories.ContentImage} />
<Controls of={ImageStories.ContentImage} />

## Properties

      <th>Property</th>

        <code>image__src</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        <code>string</code>
      </td>
      <td>Image source URL</td>
    </tr>

      <td>
        <code>string</code>
      </td>
      <td>Alternative text for accessibility (required for all images)</td>

  </tbody>
</table>

    <tr>
      <td>
        <code>content_image__width</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        <code>string</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        <code>'content'</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        Width constraint: <code>'content'</code>, <code>'highlight'</code>,{' '}
        <code>'site'</code>, or <code>'max'</code>
      </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>
        <code>image__srcset</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        <code>string</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        <code>null</code>
      </td>
      <td>Responsive image srcset for different viewport sizes</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>
        <code>image__sizes</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        <code>string</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        <code>null</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        Sizes attribute for responsive images (defines image display width at
        different breakpoints)
      </td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

## Width Options

The content image component supports four width constraints that determine how the image fits within its container:

### Content

The default width that constrains the image to the content column width. This is the standard choice for images within text content.

**Use for:**

- Images within article body content
- Inline images in blog posts
- Standard content illustrations

### Highlight

A slightly wider width that extends beyond the standard content column to emphasize the image while maintaining readability of surrounding text.

**Use for:**

- Featured images that deserve extra prominence
- Key illustrations in long-form content
- Images that benefit from additional visual space

### Site

Extends the image to the full site content width, spanning the entire content area.

**Use for:**

- Hero images
- Full-width illustrations
- Images that need maximum impact

### Max

The maximum width available, extending to the viewport edges on larger screens.

**Use for:**

- Panoramic images
- Full-bleed photography
- Images where edge-to-edge display is desired

## Image Aspect Ratios

The component supports various aspect ratios for different use cases:

- **16:9**: Wide format, ideal for hero images and landscape photography
- **3:2**: Classic photography ratio, balanced and versatile
- **4:3**: Traditional format, good for portraits and standard imagery
- **1:1**: Square format, useful for consistent grid layouts

## Technical Specifications

- **Atomic Level**: Molecule
- **Template**: `yds-content-image.twig`
- **Styles**: `_yds-content-image.scss`
- **Base Component**: Extends `yds-image.twig` (atom)

### Accessibility

- **Required alt text**: All images must have descriptive alt text
- **Empty alt for decorative images**: Use `alt=""` for purely decorative images
- **Caption semantics**: Captions use proper semantic markup (`<figcaption>`)
- **HTML structure**: Uses `<figure>` element for proper semantic structure
- **Link support**: When captions contain links, ensure sufficient color contrast

### Responsive Images

The component leverages modern responsive image techniques:

- **srcset**: Provides multiple image sources for different pixel densities and viewport sizes
- **sizes**: Tells the browser what size the image will be at different breakpoints
- **Lazy loading**: Images can be lazy-loaded for performance optimization
- **Art direction**: Different crops or compositions can be served at different breakpoints

## Usage Guidelines

### Best Practices

- Always provide meaningful alt text that describes the image content
- Use appropriate aspect ratios for your content type
- Choose width constraints that complement the surrounding content
- Add captions to provide context or attribution when helpful
- Optimize images before upload to reduce file size
- Use srcset and sizes for responsive delivery
- Consider the reading flow when placing images

### Things to Avoid

- Don't use generic alt text like "image" or "photo"
- Don't rely solely on captions to convey critical information (include in alt text)
- Avoid overly large images that slow page load
- Don't use max width indiscriminately (it can disrupt page layout)
- Avoid placing too many images too close together
- Don't forget to test image appearance across different viewport sizes

### Alt Text Guidelines

**Good alt text:**

- Describes the image content and context
- Is concise but informative
- Includes relevant details for understanding
- Considers the image's purpose in context

**Example:**
"Students collaborating in Sterling Memorial Library's reading room"

**Poor alt text:**

- "Image123.jpg"
- "Photo"
- "Click here"

## Testing

For comprehensive visual regression testing with all width variations, aspect ratios, and theme combinations, see the [Visreg page](?path=/story/molecules-image-visreg--visreg).

## Related Components

- **Image (Atom)**: The base image component
- **Wrapped Image**: Image with text wrapping for inline placement
- **Banner**: Large hero images with text overlay
